title: Casting Time Rules
excerpt: Details the rules for spell casting times, including Magic action, Bonus Action, Reaction, longer casting times, and the one spell slot per turn rule.
markdown:
# Casting Time Rules

Most spells require the [[magic_action]] to cast, but some spells require a [[bonus_action]], a [[reaction]], or 1 minute or more. A spell's Casting Time entry specifies which of those is required.

### One Spell with a Spell Slot per Turn

On a turn, you can expend only one [[spell_slot]] to cast a spell. This rule means you can't, for example, cast a spell with a [[spell_slot]] using the [[magic_action]] and another one using a [[bonus_action]] on the same turn.

### Reaction and Bonus Action Triggers

A spell that has a casting time of a [[reaction]] is cast in response to a trigger that is defined in the spell's Casting Time entry. Some spells that have a casting time of a [[bonus_action]] are also cast in response to a trigger defined in the spell.

### Longer Casting Times

Certain spells-including a spell cast as a [[ritual]]-require more time to cast: minutes or even hours. While you cast a spell with a casting time of 1 minute or more, you must take the [[magic_action]] on each of your turns, and you must maintain [[concentration]] (see the [[concentration]] rule) while you do so. If your [[concentration]] is broken, the spell fails, but you don't expend a [[spell_slot]]. To cast the spell again, you must start over.
